core.label: C++ Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, compilation, and basic program structure.

topics.label: Compilation & Toolchain
topics.description: Compile with g++/clang++, understand translation units, and linking. C++ compilation is more complex than most languages.

topics.label: Basic Types & Variables
topics.description: int, double, char, bool, auto, and const. C++ has strong typing with type deduction via auto.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else, switch, for, while, range-based for, and structured bindings. Modern C++ has cleaner iteration patterns.

topics.label: Functions & Overloading
topics.description: Define functions with overloading, default arguments, and inline. Function overloading is a key C++ feature.

topics.label: References & Pointers
topics.description: Understand lvalue references, pointers, const references, and nullptr. References are safer aliases for objects.

topics.label: Strings (std::string)
topics.description: Use std::string instead of C-style char arrays. string provides safe, convenient text manipulation.

topics.label: Arrays & std::array
topics.description: Fixed-size arrays with bounds safety. std::array is preferred over C-style arrays in modern C++.

topics.label: std::vector
topics.description: Dynamic arrays that grow automatically. vector is the most used container in C++.

topics.label: Namespaces
topics.description: Organize code into namespaces to avoid name collisions. Essential for library development and large projects.

topics.label: Enums & enum class
topics.description: Scoped enumerations (enum class) for type-safe named constants. Preferred over C-style unscoped enums.

core.label: Object-Oriented Programming
core.description: Classes, inheritance, polymorphism, and operator overloading.

topics.label: Classes & Structs
topics.description: Define classes with constructors, destructors, and member functions. Classes are C++'s primary abstraction mechanism.

topics.label: Constructors & Destructors
topics.description: Default, parameterized, copy, and move constructors plus destructors. RAII depends on proper constructor/destructor design.

topics.label: Inheritance
topics.description: Single and multiple inheritance, virtual base classes. Inheritance enables code reuse and polymorphic behavior.

topics.label: Virtual Functions & Polymorphism
topics.description: Runtime polymorphism with virtual functions, override, and pure virtual (abstract). The foundation of extensible designs.

topics.label: Operator Overloading
topics.description: Define custom behavior for +, ==, <<, [], etc. Makes user-defined types feel like built-in types.

topics.label: Copy & Move Semantics
topics.description: Rule of three/five/zero, copy/move constructors, and assignment operators. Move semantics eliminate unnecessary copies.

topics.label: RAII
topics.description: Resource Acquisition Is Initialization — tie resource lifetimes to object lifetimes. RAII is C++'s most important idiom.

topics.label: Friend Functions & Classes
topics.description: Grant access to private members selectively. Used sparingly for operator overloading and tightly coupled classes.

topics.label: Static Members
topics.description: Class-level variables and functions shared across all instances. Useful for counters, factories, and singletons.

core.label: Templates & Generic Programming
core.description: Function templates, class templates, and compile-time polymorphism.

topics.label: Function Templates
topics.description: Write type-generic functions with template<typename T>. Templates are C++'s mechanism for generic programming.

topics.label: Class Templates
topics.description: Create generic data structures and classes. The STL is built entirely on class templates.

topics.label: Template Specialization
topics.description: Provide custom implementations for specific types. Specialization handles edge cases in generic code.

topics.label: Variadic Templates
topics.description: Templates that accept any number of arguments. Powers std::tuple, std::variant, and modern library designs.

topics.label: Concepts (C++20)
topics.description: Constrain template parameters with readable requirements. Concepts replace SFINAE with clear error messages.

topics.label: constexpr & Compile-Time
topics.description: Evaluate functions and variables at compile time. constexpr enables zero-runtime-cost abstractions.

topics.label: auto & decltype
topics.description: Type deduction with auto and decltype. Reduces verbosity especially with complex template types.

topics.label: SFINAE & Type Traits
topics.description: Substitution Failure Is Not An Error — advanced template metaprogramming. Used in library code for conditional compilation.

core.label: STL Containers & Algorithms
core.description: Standard library containers, iterators, and algorithms.

topics.label: Sequence Containers
topics.description: vector, deque, list, forward_list, and array. Choose the right container based on access and insertion patterns.

topics.label: Associative Containers
topics.description: map, set, multimap, multiset (ordered) and their unordered variants. Maps and sets are essential for fast lookups.

topics.label: Iterators
topics.description: Input, output, forward, bidirectional, and random access iterators. Iterators are the glue between containers and algorithms.

topics.label: STL Algorithms
topics.description: sort, find, transform, accumulate, copy, remove_if, and more. Algorithms express intent better than raw loops.

topics.label: Lambda Expressions
topics.description: Inline anonymous functions with captures. Lambdas are used everywhere with STL algorithms and callbacks.

topics.label: Ranges (C++20)
topics.description: Composable views and range adaptors for lazy pipelines. Ranges modernize the STL algorithm interface.

topics.label: std::optional, variant, any
topics.description: Type-safe alternatives to null pointers and void*. These vocabulary types make APIs more expressive.

topics.label: std::string_view
topics.description: Non-owning string references for zero-copy string passing. Avoids unnecessary string copies in function parameters.

core.label: Memory Management & Smart Pointers
core.description: Smart pointers, ownership semantics, and memory safety.

topics.label: unique_ptr
topics.description: Exclusive ownership smart pointer that auto-deletes. Use unique_ptr by default for heap allocations.

topics.label: shared_ptr & weak_ptr
topics.description: Reference-counted shared ownership. weak_ptr breaks circular references. Use sparingly — prefer unique_ptr.

topics.label: make_unique & make_shared
topics.description: Factory functions for exception-safe smart pointer creation. Always prefer make_unique/make_shared over raw new.

topics.label: Move Semantics Deep Dive
topics.description: Rvalue references, std::move, and perfect forwarding. Move semantics are key to writing efficient modern C++.

topics.label: Custom Allocators
topics.description: Write allocators for specialized memory strategies — pools, arenas, stack allocators. Used in games and embedded.

topics.label: Placement New
topics.description: Construct objects in pre-allocated memory. Used in memory pools, custom containers, and embedded systems.

core.label: Error Handling & Concurrency
core.description: Exceptions, error patterns, and multi-threaded programming.

topics.label: Exceptions
topics.description: throw, try/catch, and exception hierarchies. C++ exceptions integrate with RAII for safe resource cleanup.

topics.label: noexcept
topics.description: Mark functions as non-throwing for optimization and documentation. Move operations should always be noexcept.

topics.label: std::expected (C++23)
topics.description: Monadic error handling as an alternative to exceptions. Represents either a value or an error.

topics.label: std::thread
topics.description: Launch and manage threads for concurrent execution. Multi-threading is essential for modern applications.

topics.label: Mutexes & Locks
topics.description: Protect shared data with mutex, lock_guard, unique_lock, and shared_lock. Prevents data races.

topics.label: Condition Variables
topics.description: Synchronize threads by waiting for conditions. Used for producer-consumer and event-driven patterns.

topics.label: Futures & Promises
topics.description: async, future, and promise for asynchronous result passing. Higher-level than raw threads for many tasks.

topics.label: Atomics
topics.description: Lock-free programming with std::atomic. Critical for high-performance concurrent data structures.

core.label: Build Systems & Testing
core.description: CMake, package managers, testing frameworks, and debugging.

topics.label: CMake
topics.description: The de facto build system for C++. Write CMakeLists.txt for cross-platform builds.

topics.label: vcpkg / Conan
topics.description: C++ package managers for dependency management. vcpkg and Conan save you from manual library building.

topics.label: Google Test
topics.description: Industry-standard C++ testing framework. Write unit tests with TEST, EXPECT, and ASSERT macros.

topics.label: Catch2
topics.description: Header-only testing framework with BDD-style syntax. Popular for its simplicity and single-header setup.

topics.label: Google Benchmark
topics.description: Microbenchmarking framework for performance-critical code. Measure before optimizing.

topics.label: Sanitizers
topics.description: AddressSanitizer, UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer, ThreadSanitizer. Compile-time flags that catch runtime bugs.

topics.label: Debugging with GDB/LLDB
topics.description: Step through C++ code, inspect objects, and trace crashes. Essential for debugging complex template errors.

topics.label: clang-format & clang-tidy
topics.description: Auto-format code and apply static analysis fixes. Enforces consistent style across C++ projects.
